title: Making the Corporate Platform Decision (ITT Hartford)
author: Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group
subject_domain: platform selection methodology; corporate IT strategy; desktop OS; workgroup server
abstract: Aberdeen Group consulting presentation for ITT Hartford on corporate platform decision-making. Background is 1994 Commercial Lines Coop review. Covers desktop OS selection (Windows 95 vs OS/2 Warp vs NT Workstation), workgroup OS strategy (Windows NT/AS and Novell NetWare), platform management, network management, groupware, electronic mail, and distributed software management. 9-slide deck.
importance_rationale: Consulting engagement for major insurance firm; demonstrates Aberdeen methodology for corporate platform decision-making; historically significant as Windows 95 era enterprise OS selection document.
relevance_rationale: Methodology and risk framework for platform decisions is still instructive; specific technology choices are historical artifacts.
prescience_rationale: Correctly selected Windows 95 as desktop OS winner; correctly predicted NT/NetWare dual workgroup-OS strategy would be pragmatic; Windows 95 indeed became dominant desktop OS, validating Aberdeen recommendation.
